summary: Backend-only live testing of Looking Glass fixes on TO.DIST (RouterOS 7.20.6 @ 157.20.32.253:8777). Created /app/backend/tests/test_looking_glass_live.py — 11 pytest cases, 11/11 PASS in 31.8s. Also re-ran /app/backend/tests/test_looking_glass.py (wire-level unit tests with FakeProtocol) — 7/7 PASS. Combined: 18/18 green. BOTH FIXES VERIFIED end-to-end against the live router. Bug 1 (BGP longest-prefix scan): POST tool=bgp_route target=103.133.20.5 now returns ok:true with a non-empty rows[] and match_prefix set to the covering CIDR (in this router's RIB the covering prefix is 0.0.0.0/0 via gw 157.20.32.254 — no specific /24 exists for that /32 today, but the containment check passes because 103.133.20.5 ∈ 0.0.0.0/0; response completed in 8.3s, well under the 20s budget). Bug 1 default route: target=0.0.0.0/0 exact-hits on the first query with match_prefix=='0.0.0.0/0'. Bug 1 invalid input: target='not-an-ip' returns HTTP 200 with {ok:false, error:'Invalid IP/prefix: not-an-ip ...'} — not 500. Bug 2 (src-address): ping to 8.8.8.8 with src_address=157.20.32.253 returns ok:true, src_address echoed, rows contain real RTTs like '14ms562us'. Traceroute to 1.1.1.1 with src_address=157.20.32.253 returns ok:true, src_address echoed, non-empty hop rows with 'address' fields. Ping without src_address still works and src_address is null in the response. Regression on 5 mikrotik ops (device test, interfaces, traffic, blackhole list, backups list) — all 200 with plausible payloads.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD — MikrotikClient._bgp_route_lookup (integrations_v2.py L293-352): correct longest-prefix scan. Uses api.rawCmd('/ip/route/print', '?dst-address=CIDR') so the router pre-filters server-side (each query is O(1) on the router side even with a full BGP table). Walks from user-supplied prefixlen (or /32 for a bare host IP) down to /0. Breaks on first hit — no wasted queries after a match. Correctly canonicalises host→network via ipaddress.ip_network(f'{base_ip}/{plen}', strict=False), so 103.133.20.5/24 is queried as 103.133.20.0/24, matching what the router stores. IPv6 support included (walks /128→/0 on /ipv6/route/print).

GOOD — invalid input handled cleanly: ipaddress.ValueError → returns ok:false with a descriptive error, no connection attempted (verified in test_bgp_route_lookup_invalid_input and against the live endpoint). Never surfaces a 500.

GOOD — MikrotikClient.looking_glass (L255-291) accepts src_address as a keyword-only argument, and only appends it to the /ping or /tool/traceroute param dict when truthy — so omitted / empty-string / None all correctly OMIT the RouterOS `src-address=` word. Prevents the classic bug where an empty string gets forwarded as `src-address=` and RouterOS rejects it.
GOOD — routes.py L3907 accepts both `src_address` and `src-address` keys in the payload and normalises to None when empty — good defensive parsing.
GOOD — response schema: match_prefix (BGP), src_address (ping/traceroute), tool, target all consistently echoed. Frontend can render a match_prefix badge deterministically.

MINOR — _bgp_route_lookup walks down /32→/0 sequentially. On a full-BGP router this is up to 33 sequential RTTs (~8s on TO.DIST for the worst case where no specific prefix exists and the walk falls through to the default). Not a bug — just observation. Could be optimised later by starting at a heuristic length (e.g., /24 for IPv4 host lookups) or issuing queries in parallel via asyncio, but the current implementation is correct and comfortably within the 20s budget.

MINOR — In _bgp_route_lookup, if the API connection is established but every rawCmd query raises, the function returns ok:true with rows=[] and match_prefix=None (same as 'no covering prefix'). Ideally, distinguish 'no cover' from 'all queries errored'. Not blocking — matches how the tests assert it, but could hide RouterOS-side query rejections during future refactors. Consider tracking an exception counter and downgrading to ok:false if 100% of queries raised.

MINOR — The looking_glass response uses `src_address: null` when omitted. Frontend AdminMikrotik.jsx should not display an empty 'Sourced from: ' line for null values — worth a visual double-check, but out of scope for this backend-only run.
seed_data_creation: None. All tests are read-only (ping/traceroute/bgp lookup / list ops). No router state modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) TO.DIST's current RIB does NOT have a specific /24 for 103.133.20.x — the longest covering prefix in the routing table is the default route 0.0.0.0/0 (gw 157.20.32.254). The bug 1 fix still passes because containment holds (any IP is inside 0.0.0.0/0). If a future test expects a specific /24 match, either seed one on the router first or pick a different target that has a specific prefix. 2) Full walk /32→/0 takes ~8s on TO.DIST (33 sequential RouterOS API RTTs). Keep test timeouts ≥15s for BGP lookups. 3) RouterOS ping 'time' field uses composite notation like '14ms562us' (14ms + 562µs) — always parse both units. 4) The blackhole and backups list endpoints return a BARE LIST (not a dict wrapper), unlike other admin mikrotik endpoints; account for that in payload shape assertions. 5) `traceroute` binary is still not baked into the container image per iteration-21 findings — the LG traceroute path uses the RouterOS API and is unaffected, but /admin/diagnostics/run tools that rely on host binaries may still fail.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (BGP lookup returns []) RCA (confirmed): the previous implementation dumped the entire routing table via api.path('ip','route') and filtered client-side with startswith(target). For a host IP like '103.133.20.5', no row's dst-address (which is stored as a CIDR string like '103.133.20.0/24' or '0.0.0.0/0') begins with '103.133.20.5' — hence rows=[] even when a covering prefix existed. Fix: replaced startswith with an ipaddress-based longest-prefix scan that issues RouterOS API queries `?dst-address=IP/LEN` from /32 down to /0. Each query is server-side filtered so the router returns at most one row. Verified live: target=103.133.20.5 → match_prefix=0.0.0.0/0 (containment true) in 8.3s. Bug 2 (ping/traceroute source interface) RCA (confirmed): looking_glass previously ignored src_address; ops couldn't probe from a specific loopback. Fix: added src_address kwarg to MikrotikClient.looking_glass; when truthy, appended to the RouterOS /ping or /tool/traceroute call as `src-address=…`. Route handler parses both `src_address` and `src-address` payload keys and normalises empty→None so the client never forwards `src-address=` with an empty value. Verified live: ping+traceroute to 8.8.8.8 / 1.1.1.1 from src=157.20.32.253 both succeed and echo src_address in the response.
